data.name: NotFoundSubtitle
data.value: This Academy article is not available.

data.name: NotFoundBody
data.value: Return to the Academy overview to choose another article.

data.name: ArticleRunningAnalysisSummary
data.value: Why running analysis needs evidence, context, and shared decisions.

data.name: ArticlePaceTrainingSummary
data.value: Why pace zones make training load controllable and understandable.

data.name: ArticlePaceTrainingBody1
data.value: Pace-controlled training translates performance data into concrete speed ranges. A plan becomes not only a distance, but a controllable training load.

data.name: ArticlePaceTrainingBody2
data.value: Pace zones help keep easy runs truly easy and hard sessions deliberately dosed. They protect against turning every session into the same medium-hard effort.

data.name: ArticlePaceTrainingBody3
data.value: PaceLetics uses reference performances, VDOT, and critical speed to make training understandable. Pace is not the goal itself; it is an orientation tool.

data.name: ArticlePaceTrainingBody4
data.value: When form, fatigue, weather, or terrain do not fit, perception still matters. Good training control combines numbers with body feel.
